report_metadata.report_title: Countering Chinese State-Sponsored Actors Compromise of Networks Worldwide to Feed Global Espionage System

campaign_name: AA25-239A – PRC Telecom & Network Espionage Campaign

http_paths.evidence_snippet: While exploiting CVE-2023-20198, the APT actors used the Web Services Management Agent WSMA endpoints webui/wsma/Http or webui/wsma/Https to bypass authentication
While exploiting CVE-2023-20198, the APT actors used the Web Services Management Agent WSMA endpoints webui/wsma/Http or webui/wsma/Https to bypass authentication

tools.name: STOWAWAY
tools.evidence_snippet: Leveraging open source multi-hop pivoting tools, such as STOWAWAY, to build chained relays for command and control (C2) and operator access, including interactive remote shells, file upload and download, SOCKS5/HTTP proxying, and local/remote port mapping with support for forward and reverse connections over encrypted node-to-node links

tools.name: cmd3 SFTP client
tools.evidence_snippet: The APT actors also use a custom SFTP client, which is a Linux binary written in Golang, to transfer encrypted archives from one location to another.

tools.name: cmd1 SFTP client
tools.evidence_snippet: cmd1 has the additional capability of collecting network packet captures on the compromised network.

tools.name: new2 SFTP client
tools.evidence_snippet: The following SFTP client binaries in Table 4 through Table 7 are similar in that they are used to transfer files from a compromised network to staging hosts where the files are prepared for exfiltration.

tools.name: sft SFTP client
tools.evidence_snippet: The following SFTP client binaries in Table 4 through Table 7 are similar in that they are used to transfer files from a compromised network to staging hosts where the files are prepared for exfiltration.

tools.name: siet.py
tools.evidence_snippet: Within Guest Shell, running Python (such as siet.py to exploit Cisco Smart Install) and native Linux tooling

cves.evidence_snippet: CVE-2024-21887 – Ivanti Connect Secure and Ivanti Policy Secure web-component command injection vulnerability
CVE-2024-3400 – Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS GlobalProtect arbitrary file creation leading to OS command injection
CVE-2023-20273 – Cisco IOS XE software web management user interface post-authentication command injection/privilege escalation
CVE-2023-20198 – Cisco IOS XE web user interface authentication bypass vulnerability
CVE-2018-0171 – Cisco IOS and IOS XE smart install remote code execution vulnerability

yara_rules.name: SALTTYPHOON_CMD1_SFTP_CLIENT
yara_rules.evidence_snippet: rule SALTTYPHOON_CMD1_SFTP_CLIENT – Detects the Salt Typhoon Cmd1 SFTP client. strings: $s1 = "monitor capture CAP"; $s2 = "export ftp%s%s%s%s"; $s3 = "main.CapExport"; $s4 = "main.SftpDownload"; $s5 = ".SSHClient.CommandShell"; $aes = "aes.decryptBlockGo"; $buildpath = "C:\work\sync\v1\cmd\cmd1\main.go"

yara_rules.name: SALTTYPHOON_NEW2_SFTP_CLIENT
yara_rules.evidence_snippet: rule SALTTYPHOON_NEW2_SFTP_CLIENT – Detects the Salt Typhoon New2 SFTP client. strings: $set1_1 = "invoke_shell"; $set1_2 = "execute_commands"; $set2_1 = "COMMANDS_FILE"; $set2_2 = "RUNTIME"; $set3_1 = "commands.log"; $set3_2 = "Executing command"; $set3_4 = "Network sniffer script"; $set3_5 = "tar -czvf - %s | openssl des3 -salt -k password -out %s.tar.gz"; $setrequired = { 70 61 72 61 6D 69 6B 6F }

sigma_rules.name: CVE-2023-20198 Snort Detection Rule
sigma_rules.evidence_snippet: alert tcp any any -> any $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"Potential CVE-2023-20198 exploit attempt - HTTP Request to Add Privilege 15 User Detected"; content:"POST"; http_method; pcre:"/webui\/wsma|%2577eb%2575i%2577sma/i"; http_uri; content:"request xmlns=\"urn:cisco:wsma-config-correlator\">\"exec\""; http_client_body; content:"<configApply details=\"all\""; content:"privilege 15"; sid:1000003; rev:1;)

summary: CISA Cybersecurity Advisory AA25-239A (v1.1, 3 September 2025), co-sealed by NSA, CISA, FBI, DC3, and 18 international partner agencies, details PRC state-sponsored APT activity (overlapping with Salt Typhoon, OPERATOR PANDA, RedMike, UNC5807, GhostEmperor) targeting telecommunications, government, transportation, lodging, and military infrastructure networks globally since at least 2021. Operations are linked to Sichuan Juxinhe Network Technology Co. Ltd., Beijing Huanyu Tianqiong Information Technology Co. Ltd., and Sichuan Zhixin Ruijie Network Technology Co. Ltd., which provide cyber services to the PLA and MSS. Actors exploit publicly known CVEs (CVE-2023-20198, CVE-2023-20273, CVE-2024-21887, CVE-2024-3400, CVE-2018-0171) for initial access, then persist via ACL modification, SSH key injection, non-standard ports, and on-box Linux containers (Cisco Guest Shell). Lateral movement relies on SNMP enumeration, TACACS/RADIUS credential capture via native PCAP, and routing manipulation. Custom Golang SFTP tools (cmd1, cmd3, new2, sft) and STOWAWAY multi-hop proxy are used for exfiltration over GRE/IPsec tunnels. 85 actor-associated IP addresses (August 2021–June 2025) and four custom tool hashes are provided as IOCs alongside YARA rules and a Snort detection rule for CVE-2023-20198 exploitation.

timeline.event: Earliest exploited CVE in scope: CVE-2018-0171 (Cisco IOS/IOS XE Smart Install RCE)
timeline.evidence_snippet: CVE-2018-0171 - Cisco IOS and IOS XE smart install remote code execution vulnerability

timeline.event: Earliest observed APT actor IP address activity (first IP indicators recorded)
timeline.evidence_snippet: The following IP indicators were associated with the APT actors activity from August 2021 to June 2025. Several of these observed IP addresses were first observed as early as August 2021.

timeline.event: APT actors begin performing malicious operations globally (earliest known campaign activity)
timeline.evidence_snippet: The APT actors have been performing malicious operations globally since at least 2021.

timeline.event: CVE-2023-20198 and CVE-2023-20273 exploited for initial access and privilege escalation on Cisco IOS XE devices
timeline.evidence_snippet: CVE-2023-20198 - Cisco IOS XE web user interface authentication bypass vulnerability. CVE-2023-20273 - Cisco IOS XE software web management user interface post-authentication command injection/privilege escalation commonly chained with CVE-2023-20198 for initial access to achieve code execution as root.

timeline.event: CVE-2024-21887 and CVE-2024-3400 exploited against Ivanti and Palo Alto Networks edge devices
timeline.evidence_snippet: CVE-2024-21887 - Ivanti Connect Secure and Ivanti Policy Secure web-component command injection vulnerability, commonly chained after CVE-2023-46805 authentication bypass. CVE-2024-3400 - Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS GlobalProtect arbitrary file creation leading to OS command injection.

timeline.event: cmd1 custom SFTP client binary compiled (version string V20240816)
timeline.evidence_snippet: cmd1 SFTP client – Version String V20240816; Build Path String C:\work\sync\v1\cmd\cmd1\main.go

timeline.event: Last date of observed APT actor IP address activity included in IOC table
timeline.evidence_snippet: The following IP indicators were associated with the APT actors activity from August 2021 to June 2025.
Version History: 27 August 2025, v1.0 Initial publication

timeline.event: Advisory AA25-239A v1.1 published with Japan NCO name correction, clarified CVE ordering, one IP correction, and two IP removals
timeline.evidence_snippet: Version History: 3 September 2025, v1.1 Japan NCO name correction, added introduction in Technical details, update in Initial access to clarify example CVEs ordering, one IP correction and two removals.
